title: Stabilize full Python suite on local Python 3.14/macOS
description: During project-LICHEN-py4c.1.16 validation, affected slices and CI-targeted commands passed, but the local macOS Python 3.14 full-suite command timed out after reaching 82 percent. A reviewer also saw out-of-scope full-suite issues on this host around Renode port reuse and DirectedRadio cad support. Reproduce with verbose test names, isolate the hang/failures, and either fix Python 3.14/macOS compatibility or explicitly constrain supported local Python versions.
notes: No-hardware implementation evidence (2026-07-03): stabilized the local full Python suite by making the external Renode subprocess integration test opt-in via LICHEN_RUN_RENODE_INTEGRATION=1. The default suite still runs Renode server/unit coverage, but no longer launches Renode during normal pytest collection/runs. test_renode_integration.py now uses tmp_path for generated .resc scripts, OS-assigned ephemeral lichen-sim bridge ports, an ephemeral Renode monitor port, required LichenSubGHz/STM32WL_RCC/STM32WL_PWR Renode peripheral includes, a bound observer callback, explicit Renode return-code diagnostics, post-timeout process kill plus pipe drain, and a real TX assertion tx_received == [("renode-node", 4)]. This removes fixed port 5555/1234 collisions and source-tree generated file churn while preserving opt-in Renode validation.\nValidation evidence (2026-07-03, local no-hardware/simulator): cd python && uv sync --python 3.14 --locked --all-extras --dev => synced Python 3.14.5 env. cd python && uv run python -V && uv run pytest -q --timeout=120 --timeout-method=thread => Python 3.14.5; 2195 passed, 5 skipped in 49.13s. cd python && uv run ruff check tests/sim/test_renode_integration.py => All checks passed. cd python && uv run ruff check src tests pyproject.toml => All checks passed. cd python && uv run pytest -q -rs tests/sim/test_renode_integration.py tests/sim/test_renode_server.py => 2 passed, 1 skipped; skip reason set LICHEN_RUN_RENODE_INTEGRATION=1. cd python && LICHEN_RUN_RENODE_INTEGRATION=1 uv run pytest -q tests/sim/test_renode_integration.py => 1 passed in 3.11s. git diff --check => passed. Negative evidence resolved: prior default full suite failed on Renode fixed port 5555; opt-in run then exposed Renode monitor 1234 collision and missing RCC/PWR includes; both fixed.\nReview evidence: Final Lagrange review reported no P0/P1/P2 findings; verified default skip avoids Renode probing, opt-in docs, ephemeral ports, RCC/PWR/SubGHz includes, bound observer, timeout drain, tmp_path script, and TX assertion. Final Feynman review reported no findings; default skip path and opt-in Renode integration passed, diff check clean. Final Hegel review reported no P0/P1/P2 findings; default full suite passed in uv env (2195 passed, 5 skipped), opt-in Renode integration passed, no lingering Renode process/listen socket observed. Earlier P1/P2 review findings around timeout cleanup, stale docs, observer binding, import-time probe, monitor port 1234, and missing custom peripheral includes were fixed before final reviews.
close_reason: Completed: Python 3.14/macOS full suite passes in locked uv environment; Renode subprocess integration is opt-in with ephemeral ports/temp script and validated separately.
